STAGE 4: REAL LIFE Once character has finished school ad training; the real challenge making living begins_ Depending on your earlier path choices your character has several different options at this stage As with the Higher Education Stage the Real Life Stage is optional; however, most characters should go through it at least once or they will be totally green when play begins Time: Real Life Paths take variable amounts of time  depending on the contents of each path The number of years each path takes is listed under its Time heading: Repeats: After completing path in this stage characters who meet the prerequisites may continue in that path, move difterent Real Life Path or return to Stage 3: Higher Education_ following the Next Path restrictions as usual. After completing any Real Life Path_ player may end his charac ter" Life Path and proceed t0 Assigning Attributes_ Traits and Skills 54). Clan Characters: Trueborn warriors must follow the Tour of Duty: Clan Path. Freeborn warriors can choose To Serve and Protect instead_ Scientist caste characters can choose Civilian Job or Postgraduate Studies; technicians can choose Civilian Job or serve 0n the front lines Tour of Duty: Clan; most mer- chants will follow the Free Trader Path but can also choose Civilian Job; and aborer caste characters toil away at the Civilian Job Path, with no other prospects_
Mandatory Subpath: Other Majors University Path required Create your Own major using the following guidelines Tlme: years Skllls:   Academic / Any Relevant 4 add any two skills: Choose three: Academic / Any +3, Acting Art / Any Relevant +3, Administration Career / Any Relevant Computers +2, Language; Any +3 or Training +3 Events: Do not roll events for Other Majors Next Path: Civilian Job (4), Ne'er-Do-Well (4), Postgraduate Studies (41

CIVILIAN JOB Your job in the private sector may be dull; but countless trillions of civilians do this t0 make living and keep the war machines of the Inner Sphere going: This path also represents non-combat civil-service jobs Because this path covers SO many possible careers the player chooses his or her character'$ job ad relevant sub- skills with the gamemaster'$ approval.
